title: Crispy Caramelized German Onion Tart
description: A yeasty, crumbly tart with caramelized onions and butter, low-glycemic and crowd-pleasing, served in a bento box.
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine 250g spelt flour, 5g active dry yeast, and 1 tsp sea salt.
Add 150ml warm water and knead until the dough is elastic, about 8-10 minutes.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let it rise in a warm place for 45 minutes, or until doubled in size.
While the dough rises, slice 2 medium onions and heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and 2 tbsp erythritol, sautéing until caramelized and golden brown, about 15 minutes, stirring frequently.
Preheat the oven to 200 C and line a 9-inch tart pan with parchment paper.
Punch down the dough, roll it out to fit the tart pan, press it into the crust and crimp the edges.
Spread the caramelized onions evenly over the crust, drizzle with softened butter, and sprinkle fresh thyme on top.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is golden and the butter is bubbling.
Remove from the oven, let cool for 10 minutes, then cut into slices, place in a bento box, and serve.
